summary: Completed full Playwright E2E sweep with selector hardening for responsive toolbar contracts; final run is green (46/46 passed).
how_to_test: make test-e2e && cd e2e && API_PORT=8001 WEB_PORT=3001 npx playwright test tests/pdf-reader.spec.ts --grep "upload -> viewer -> persistent highlight -> send to chat" --repeat-each=2
risks: Search UI now fails closed on legacy flat payloads; backend response contract drift will surface immediately as dropped rows.
Transcript polling is strictly transcript_state-driven; backend state inconsistency (extracting without queued/running state) can suppress client polling until fixed server-side.
PDF quote-to-chat E2E remains one of the most timing-sensitive paths (upload + reload + pane routing); selector hardening reduced flakes, but nondeterminism risk under host load is not zero.
